page: Amazon Web Services z1d.6xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8151 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 24 vCPUs across 12 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a high clock speed of 4.0 GHz and is equipped with a 24 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, and AES cryptography.

The instance is provisioned with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. There are no GPUs bundled with this configuration, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the z1d.6xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, with its PassMark memory score landing in the top 25% tier. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 1457.41 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 107.83 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks. CPU performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 26,203.53 and a single-core score of 1,657.99. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 24.54 million operations per second and a PassMark database score of 9,292.43.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-core compute speeds combined with substantial memory capacity. The inclusion of fast local NVMe storage makes it well-suited for relational databases, electronic design automation, high-performance computing tasks, and memory-intensive data processing. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training, as evidenced by its poor performance in large-model LLM inference benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.6xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a 12-core Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at 4.0 GHz, delivering 24 vCPUs with dedicated allocation. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In performance testing, the instance exhibits strong memory capabilities, achieving a top-tier PassMark memory score and a cached read bandwidth of 1457.41 GB/sec. CPU benchmarks show balanced multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 26,203.53. Lacking GPU hardware, the z1d.6xlarge is optimized for high-frequency single-threaded tasks, relational databases, electronic design automation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads rather than large-scale machine learning.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 24 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD for high-frequency database and EDA workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 192 GB RAM, and 900 GB NVMe SSD local storage.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8151 processor with 24 vCPUs running at a high 4.0 GHz clock speed
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks
Best suited for relational databases, electronic design automation, and memory-intensive workloads
